title: Legibility proofing + accessibility-expert + lived-experience review
context: The Elyos quality bar for the M1 accessibility family requires specialist and lived-experience sign-off before release. After the glyphs are drawn (fonts-design-102), the family must be reviewed by an accessibility/vision specialist and at least two lived-experience reviewers (low-vision and/or dyslexic readers). This task coordinates that review and documents its outcome honestly, including any null or mixed findings.
objective: Coordinate and document legibility proofing and human review of the drawn Latin family by an accessibility/vision specialist and ≥2 lived-experience reviewers (low-vision and/or dyslexic readers), recording sign-off or feedback-driven changes, verifying non-medical framing, and reporting any null or mixed findings honestly.
acceptanceCriteria: Accessibility/vision specialist sign-off is recorded; the non-medical framing text ('designed using published legibility research; not a treatment, not a diagnosis, not a medical device') is verified present in README, specimen, and usage guide.
At least two lived-experience reviewers (low-vision and/or dyslexic readers) provide consented, aggregate feedback; no disability status is tied to an identifiable person in any recorded output.
Any feedback-driven glyph changes are logged with rationale; if changes are made, fonts-design-102 is updated accordingly.
If validation results are null or mixed, the result is reported honestly and the family is reframed as 'high-legibility, openly licensed' rather than suppressing the finding.
Review report is CC-BY-4.0; reviewer names appear only with their explicit consent; pseudonyms honored.
No health, disability, or personally identifiable information is published; all reviewer data is aggregate and de-identified.
resources: PLAN.md (section 4: success metrics — O8 honesty clause)
PLAN.md (section 8: required reviews before a deed is done)
output: A review report document (CC-BY-4.0) recording accessibility specialist and lived-experience reviewer sign-off (or honest null/mixed results), the non-medical framing verification, and any feedback-driven design changes, merged into the repository.
